number: NNN
title: <Short imperative title — "Use PostgreSQL for X" not "PostgreSQL Decision">
status: proposed  # proposed | accepted | superseded by ADR-NNN | deprecated

# ADR-NNN: <Short imperative title>

## Context

The technical situation that calls for a decision. State the forces at play: constraints, requirements, existing systems, performance/security/cost considerations. Someone reading this in two years should understand *why this decision had to be made*, not just *what it was*.

Keep this section factual. Save advocacy for "Decision" and "Consequences".

## Decision

The decision, stated clearly and in active voice ("We will use X for Y because Z"). One paragraph is usually enough. If the decision has sub-parts, list them.

## Consequences

What becomes true once this decision is in effect — both positive and negative. Include:

- **Positive:** What we gain.
- **Negative:** What we lose or have to live with.
- **Neutral:** Trade-offs that are neither clearly good nor bad but worth knowing.

Be honest about the negatives. A future engineer evaluating whether to override this ADR needs to know what tradeoffs were already weighed.

## Considered alternatives

Each alternative gets a brief paragraph: what it was, and why it wasn't chosen. The point is to leave a trail showing the decision wasn't made in a vacuum.

### Alternative 1: <name>

Brief description. Why not chosen.

### Alternative 2: <name>

Brief description. Why not chosen.

## Notes

Optional. Anything that doesn't fit above: links to spike code, benchmarks, prior discussions, follow-up ADRs anticipated.
